LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF APPEALS AND THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CPF-12-512623 WITH CASE CPF-11-511369 IS DENIED. THE TWO ACTIONS INVOLVE TWO SEPARATE ADMINISTRATIVE ACTIONS INVOLVING TWO SEPARATE PERMITS. RESPONDENTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THE TWO CASES CONCERN A COMMON QUESTION OF LAW OR FACT SUCH THAT JUDICIAL ECONOMY WOULD BE SERVED BY CONSOLIDATING THEM. THE COURT ORDERS PETITIONER TO CALENDAR THE HEARING ON IT WRIT IN CASE #511369 BY DECEMBER 31, 2013, OR SHOW CAUSE WHY THAT IS NOT FEASIBLE. THE PARTIES SHOULD NOTE THAT IN CASE 512623 THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, A COURTESY COPY OF THE PETITION OR A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION BRIEF. SEE LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE PARTIES MUST PROVIDE THESE DOCUMENTS TO DEPARTMENT 302 BY NO LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 23, 2013 AT 4:00 P.M. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. PETITIONER CROWN CASTLE NG WEST INC.'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS OR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 26, 2014 TO GIVE PETITIONER THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6C. PETITIONER MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE PETITION TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 5, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE ANSWER, MOVING, OPPOSITION, AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-26-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING BUT MODIFIED THE LAST SENTENCE PER THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT. PETITIONER CROWN CASTLE NG WEST INC.'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS OR WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. ALL REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. RESPONDENTS ABUSED THEIR DISCRETION IN CONSIDERING THE LATE-FILED APPEAL TO THE DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS' ISSUANCE OF WIRELESS BOX PERMIT NO. 10WR-0021. THE RECORD SHOWS THAT THE APPEAL PERIOD HAD EXPIRED, AND THE CITY HAD NOT INTENTIONALLY OR INADVERTENTLY CAUSE THE REQUESTOR TO BE LATE IN FILING THE APPEAL. (SEE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD, 1; SEE ALSO PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, EXH, C, RULES OF THE BOARD OF APPEALS, SECTION 10(A). UNDER THEN-EXISTING LAW, THE LACK OF NOTICE WAS NOT GROUNDS FOR ACCEPTANCE OF AN UNTIMELY APPEAL. WIRELESS BOX PERMIT NO. 10WR-0021, WHICH THE BOARD'S DECISION INAPPROPRIATELY REVOKED, SHALL REMAIN IN EFFECT, AND THE BOARD'S DECISION IS VOID. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Or Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Sam N. Dawood, Esq. of Davis Wright Tremaine LLP, Tel. 415.276.6566, for petitioner and moving party;
William Sanders, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.6771, for respondent and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling but modified the last sentence per the parties' agreement. PETITIONER CROWN CASTLE NG WEST INC.'s Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Or Writ Of Mandate is Granted. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Respondents abused their discretion in considering the late-filed appeal to the Department of Public Works' issuance of Wireless Box Permit No. 10WR-0021. The record shows that the appeal period had expired, and the City had not intentionally or inadvertently cause the requestor to be late in filing the appeal. (See Administrative Record, 1; see also Petitioner's Request For Judicial Notice, Exh, C, Rules of the Board of Appeals, Section 10(a). Under then-existing law, the lack of notice was not grounds for acceptance of an untimely appeal. WIRELESS BOX PERMIT NO. 10WR-0021, WHICH THE BOARD'S DECISION INAPPROPRIATELY REVOKED, SHALL REMAIN IN EFFECT, AND THE BOARD'S DECISION IS VOID. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Respondents The City And County Of San Francisco, The City And County Of San Francisco Board of Appeals and The City And County Of San Francisco's Motion To Consolidate Actions Of Case(S) Cpf-12-512623 With Case Cpf-11-511369 is denied. The two actions involve two separate administrative actions involving two separate permits. Respondents have not shown that the two cases concern a common question of law or fact such that judicial economy would be served by consolidating them. The Court orders Petitioner to calendar the hearing on it writ in case #511369 by December 31, 2013, or show cause why that is not feasible. The parties should note that in case 512623 the Court does not have the administrative record, a courtesy copy of the petition or a courtesy copy of the opposition brief. See Local Rule 2.6B. The parties must provide these documents to Department 302 by no later than September 23, 2013 at 4:00 p.m. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/mj

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CPF-12-512623 WITH CASE CPF-11-511369
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS and THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF APPEALS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CPF-12-512623 WITH CASE CPF-11-511369 is continued to September 20, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 per ex parte order. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, pursuant to Local Rule 2.6B, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than September 18, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
